Believing that the real news of
the labor world is of the greatest
Importance to all and that little of
this news reaches them through
other channels. The Press has ar
ranged with Max S. Hayes, noted
as an authority on labor conditions,
to write from time to time articles
dealing with the big things in the
labor market. Herewith Mr.. Hayes
first article:


BY Max 8. Hayes


The prestige Of the miners feder-
alton in Great Britain, of being the
largest trade union in the world,
with its $4,000 members, is likely
to be eclipsed soon.


The middle of next month the
Western Federation of Miners will
hold its annual convention in Den
yer, at which time the proposition
OF combining with the United Mine
Workers Of America will be consid-
cred. Indications are that favor
able action will be taken.





When the u. M. w. met In In
dianapolis last January. w. d. Hay
wood, formerly secretary OF the
Western Federation, delivered an
address and suggested an amalga-
nation of the two international
unions, The sentiment was warmly
received and committee was elect
ed to attend the coming convention
in Denver and continue negotia-
tons to merge the two organiza-
lions.


If the deal goes through the COM
bination will start out with fully
100000 men, exclusive OF at least
100000 nonunion anthracite miners.


During the past week President
Lewis the United Mine Workers
has been busily planning cam
paign OF organization in the three
anthracite districts, and it Is be-
lieved he will he successful In ac-
compllshing his object, as the agree
ment with the operators expires the
first OF next April.


Lewis is ambitious to gather all
classes OF miners on the American
continent into one huge union, and
as the Western Federation domi'
mates in metalliferous mining. an
amalgamation of the coal and metal
miners would, in the language OF
Haywood, give the miners as com
plete control In their industry as
the typographical union has in the
printing trade


Haywood believes that inside OF
year between 100000 and 100000
miners In the United States, Can
ada and Mexico would be enrolled
In the federated union,
